approaches.approach: A. Server-side orchestration via MCP sampling (ctx.sample / sampling/createMessage with modelPreferences)
approaches.how_it_works: A FastMCP tool (e.g. swarm_run) would call ctx.sample() per role: haiku-hinted requests for reddit/new-listing polling, sonnet-hinted for news, opus-hinted for TA, then the host model fuses and the tool places the order via the existing paper_* code paths. The server itself drives every LLM call; the host is just a dumb sampling executor. modelPreferences (hints: [{name:'haiku'}], costPriority/speedPriority/intelligencePriority) is the spec's per-role routing mechanism.
approaches.model_routing: Per-call modelPreferences on each sampling/createMessage request: name hints ('haiku','sonnet','opus') plus cost/speed/intelligence priorities. The CLIENT maps hints to actual models — hints are advisory substring matches, not guarantees.
approaches.looping: A server-side asyncio loop (or APScheduler) re-runs the swarm on an interval inside a long-lived tool call or background task, with the standing goal and PnL journal stored server-side.
approaches.pros: Architecturally purest: 100% of orchestration lives in the MCP server; works identically under any sampling-capable client; zero repo-shipped client assets; the spec's modelPreferences gives exactly the haiku/sonnet/opus routing the vision describes.
approaches.cons: DEAD ON ARRIVAL TODAY: Claude Code does not implement sampling (anthropics/claude-code#1785, open since June 2025, still open June 2026) and Claude Desktop does not either (modelcontextprotocol.io client-concepts: 'not yet supported in the Claude Desktop client'). FastMCP's ctx.sample() would error; its sampling_handler fallback (FastMCP >= 2.12) calls a completions API directly — which requires an Anthropic/OpenAI API key, violating the no-API-billing constraint. Keep ctx.sample code paths dormant for the day #1785 ships, but you cannot build on this now.

approaches.approach: B. RECOMMENDED — MCP server ships the playbook (prompt + tools + state); repo-shipped Claude Code subagents do the model routing; host (fable) fuses and trades; /loop or scheduled headless claude -p drives the interval
approaches.how_it_works: The app stays a pure FastMCP server with three kinds of pieces: (1) TOOLS — existing read tools (reddit/new-listings poller, news fetch, crypto_decide, crypto_screen) plus paper_* write tools, plus new swarm-state tools: swarm_goal_get/set (standing goal), swarm_journal_append/read (decisions + PnL per cycle, persisted to disk so state survives across headless runs); (2) a PROMPT @mcp.prompt('trade_swarm') — the orchestration playbook, surfaced in Claude Code as /mcp__makecrazypenny__trade_swarm. It instructs the HOST: 'read swarm_goal and last journal entries; spawn listing-scout, news-reader, chart-analyst subagents in parallel; fuse their reports; decide; if conviction >= threshold call paper_trade_decision; append the cycle to swarm_journal'; (3) optional MCP RESOURCES exposing the journal/goal for @-mention. The repo additionally ships .claude/agents/listing-scout.md (model: haiku, tools allowlisted to the cheap polling MCP tools + WebSearch), news-reader.md (model: sonnet), chart-analyst.md (model: opus, tools: crypto_decide/factor tools). All three have paper_* write tools EXCLUDED via tools:/disallowedTools: frontmatter, so only the host session (fable, the frontier model) can place trades — enforcing the fusion-then-trade hierarchy structurally. The host invokes them via the Task tool (subagents inherit the MCP connection, run in isolated contexts, return only summaries — cheap on the main context). This is exactly the documented pattern: subagent docs explicitly list 'Control costs by routing tasks to faster, cheaper models like Haiku'.
approaches.model_routing: Per-role 'model:' YAML frontmatter in .claude/agents/*.md — verified at code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents: accepts aliases sonnet, opus, haiku, fable, a full model ID, or inherit (default). Resolution order: CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL env var > per-invocation model parameter on the Task call > frontmatter > main-conversation model. The host session itself runs fable (claude --model fable or the default), giving haiku=scout, sonnet=news, opus=TA, fable=fusion+execution. All inference bills to the Claude Code subscription.
approaches.looping: Three subscription-billed options, layered: (1) interactive: the /loop pattern (loop skill) re-invokes /mcp__makecrazypenny__trade_swarm every N minutes in a live session — best while supervising; (2) unattended: Windows Task Scheduler runs headless 'claude -p "/mcp__makecrazypenny__trade_swarm" --permission-mode acceptEdits' on a schedule — uses Claude Code CLI subscription auth, no API key; before June 15 2026 it counts against plan limits, after June 15 it draws from the new monthly Agent SDK credit ($20 Pro / $100 Max 5x / $200 Max 20x) and no longer touches interactive limits. Each run is stateless, which is why the standing goal + journal MUST live server-side (swarm_goal/swarm_journal tools), not in conversation memory; (3) push-driven: the server declares the claude/channel capability and pushes 'new listing detected' events into a session started with --channels, so Claude reacts between scheduled cycles. Ship a .claude/settings.json allowlisting mcp__makecrazypenny__* read tools so headless runs never stall on permission prompts; gate paper_trade_decision behind FastMCP elicitation (ctx.elicit) — Claude Code renders a confirm dialog interactively, and the Elicitation hook can auto-approve in headless mode under explicit risk rules.
approaches.pros: Every piece works TODAY and is verified against current docs; zero API key — subagents, Task tool, /loop, and headless claude -p are all subscription-billed; exact 4-tier model routing via documented frontmatter; the server remains pure MCP (tools/prompts/resources/elicitation/channel) and still works with weaker clients (the prompt degrades to single-model execution); tool allowlists make 'only the frontier model trades' a hard guarantee, not a convention; server-side journal gives durable PnL tracking across stateless headless cycles; channels + elicitation cover alerting and human-in-the-loop confirmation.
approaches.cons: Orchestration intelligence is split: the playbook text lives in the server prompt but the role definitions live in repo-shipped .claude/agents/ files — a non-Claude-Code client gets the prompt but not the model routing; the host must faithfully follow the playbook (mitigate with a strict checklist-style prompt and journal-append verification); headless scheduled runs consume plan limits until June 15 2026, so pick a sane interval (e.g. 15-30 min) until the credit bucket lands; subagents cannot be given a DIFFERENT MCP server config than the host without extra frontmatter (mcpServers field exists if needed).

approaches.approach: C. Python Agent SDK driver loop (separate process beside the MCP server)
approaches.how_it_works: A small Python runner (not part of the server) uses claude-agent-sdk to run the cycle programmatically: query(prompt='/mcp__makecrazypenny__trade_swarm', options with mcp_servers={makecrazypenny: stdio}, agents={...} defined inline with per-agent model). The SDK authenticates through the installed Claude Code CLI using subscription credentials — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (verified: support.claude.com article 15036540; from June 15 2026 it draws from the monthly Agent SDK credit). A while/asyncio loop with sleep(interval) re-runs it; goal/PnL still read/written through the server's swarm_goal/swarm_journal tools.
approaches.model_routing: AgentDefinition(model='haiku'|'sonnet'|'opus') per subagent in ClaudeAgentOptions.agents — same routing vocabulary as frontmatter, defined in code; top-level options.model='fable' for the fusion host.
approaches.looping: Native: a real Python scheduler loop (asyncio/APScheduler/Windows service) with full control over interval, backoff, kill-switches, and PnL-based stop conditions — the most robust unattended loop of the three.
approaches.pros: Strongest loop ergonomics (retries, jitter, circuit-breaker on drawdown); fully unattended; subscription-billed (Agent SDK credit after June 15 2026); reuses the identical MCP server and even the same trade_swarm prompt, so it's purely additive to Approach B.
approaches.cons: The orchestrator is now a second long-running process — the app is no longer ONLY an MCP server (mitigation: ship it as an optional console script, e.g. makecrazypenny-swarm, that merely drives the unchanged server); requires Claude Code CLI installed on the box; before June 15 2026 heavy loops eat interactive plan limits; duplicate agent definitions vs .claude/agents/ unless you generate one from the other.

approaches.approach: D. Scheduled cloud agents (schedule skill / routines) or cron-triggered cloud sessions
approaches.how_it_works: Claude Code's scheduled cloud agents run a standing prompt on a cron schedule in Anthropic's cloud on the subscription. The routine's prompt would be the trade_swarm playbook.
approaches.model_routing: Same subagent mechanism would apply if the cloud session can reach the MCP server; routine itself runs one configured model.
approaches.looping: Best-in-class scheduling UX (cron expressions, managed runs, mobile notifications) with zero local infrastructure.
approaches.pros: No local machine needs to stay on; native cron; subscription-billed.
approaches.cons: FATAL FOR THIS APP AS-IS: makecrazypenny is a local stdio MCP server holding MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY locally — cloud agents cannot spawn your local stdio process. Only viable if the server is re-deployed as a remote HTTP MCP server with OAuth, which contradicts keeping secrets local and adds hosting. Use only as a future option; local Task Scheduler (Approach B) or local cron tools cover the same need.

recommendation: Build Approach B now; keep A dormant and C as an optional runner. Concretely: (1) IN THE MCP SERVER (C:\Users\persi\Desktop\MCPenny\makecrazypenny\mcp_server.py — prompts already live at lines 1017-1111): add @mcp.prompt('trade_swarm') as the orchestration playbook (spawn listing-scout/news-reader/chart-analyst in parallel via Task, fuse, decide, trade via paper_trade_decision, journal the cycle), plus swarm_goal_get/set and swarm_journal_append/read tools persisting standing goal + per-cycle PnL to disk (this is what makes stateless headless loops coherent), plus ctx.elicit() confirmation before any order, and optionally the claude/channel capability for push alerts. (2) IN REPO-SHIPPED CLAUDE CODE ASSETS: .claude/agents/listing-scout.md (model: haiku), .claude/agents/news-reader.md (model: sonnet), .claude/agents/chart-analyst.md (model: opus) — each with a tools: allowlist of read-only MCP tools and paper_* explicitly excluded so only the fable host can trade; .claude/commands/trade-swarm.md as a thin wrapper invoking the MCP prompt; .claude/settings.json allowlisting mcp__makecrazypenny__* read tools so unattended runs never stall. (3) THE LOOP: interactively, /loop 15m /mcp__makecrazypenny__trade_swarm; unattended, Windows Task Scheduler running claude -p with the same slash command (subscription auth; after June 15 2026 this draws from the new monthly Agent SDK credit instead of plan limits — set the interval conservatively until then). This is the only architecture where every component is verified working today, the app stays a pure FastMCP server, roles route exactly haiku/sonnet/opus/fable, the goal persists server-side, and not one token bills to an API key. Do NOT build on MCP sampling: neither Claude Code nor Claude Desktop supports it (see mcp_sampling_status), and FastMCP's fallback path requires the very API key you're avoiding.
mcp_sampling_status: NOT SUPPORTED by either client as of June 2026 — verified three ways. (1) anthropics/claude-code issue #1785 ('Support for MCP Sampling to leverage Claude Max subscriptions'), opened June 8 2025, fetched directly: still OPEN, labels area:mcp/area:cost/enhancement, assigned but unimplemented; an MCP server connected to Claude Code that needs LLM inference must call the Anthropic API itself. (2) Claude Code's own MCP reference (code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp, fetched in full) enumerates supported client-side MCP features — tools, prompts as /mcp__server__prompt slash commands, resources via @-mentions, elicitation dialogs (with an Elicitation hook for auto-respond), list_changed notifications, and channels (claude/channel push capability) — and sampling appears nowhere in the document. (3) The official MCP docs (modelcontextprotocol.io client concepts/example clients) state sampling 'is not yet supported in the Claude Desktop client'. Consequence: FastMCP's ctx.sample()/ctx.session.create_message() would fail against both hosts; FastMCP >= 2.12 offers a sampling_handler fallback, but it satisfies the request by calling a completions API directly (pip install fastmcp[anthropic]) — i.e., it requires an API key and defeats the subscription-only goal. modelPreferences (name hints like 'haiku', costPriority/speedPriority/intelligencePriority) is real in the spec (2025-11-25 revision) and would be the right routing mechanism if/when #1785 ships, so it is reasonable to leave an optional ctx.sample code path behind a capability check — but per-role model routing today must come from Claude Code subagent 'model:' frontmatter instead.
